tasks.prompt: Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C connector before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C connector to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
For AP2112 voltage regulator, identify input, output, and ground, then measure no-short, output voltage, load current, and thermal behavior.
For CH340C USB-to-Serial bridge IC, measure continuity from USB pins to the USB connector and from UART pins to candidate headers before treating it as a USB serial adapter.
Identify TX, RX, GND, and VCC on DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header; measure idle logic high and run loopback or safe serial capture before target connection.

target.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

tasks.prompt: Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) ground reference continuity: 6:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) logic voltage domain: 1:DTR, 2:RXI, 3:TXO, 5:CTS. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) signal pin continuity map: 1:DTR, 2:RXI, 3:TXO, 5:CTS. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) supply voltage and polarity: 4:VCC. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) supply-to-ground no-short: 4:VCC vs 6:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) ground reference continuity: GND:GND, SHLD:SHLD. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) supply voltage and polarity: VBUS:VBUS. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) supply-to-ground no-short: VBUS:VBUS vs GND:GND, SHLD:SHLD. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.

production_casefile.claim_boundary: This casefile assembles evidence and authority state. It does not turn model output into repair authority; production release requires measured evidence, terminal outcome, and a passing authority ledger.

design_test_kit.claim_boundary: This test kit can validate requirements, module contracts, evidence gates, and supplied DC power-tree netlists. It does not authorize arbitrary unknown boards, mains work, battery packs, or production release without measured topology, bench evidence, and specialist review where hazards apply.

compiled_topology_netlist.claim_boundary: Compiled netlists cover low-voltage measured rails, explicit load models, optional trace/regulator models, and voltage constraints only. Hidden nets, high-speed behavior, batteries, mains, and unmeasured loads remain outside this deterministic DC model.

issues.detail: The compiler emitted rail constraints, but source-current and loaded voltage behavior need measured current or explicit load models.
issues.summary: No numeric load-current model was found.

load_envelope.reason: No source current limit was supplied, so the compiler cannot derive a bounded load envelope without guessing.

topology_authority.claim_boundary: Public reference topology can guide tests but does not prove this physical board.

required_measurements: source voltage and current limit
no-short resistance from each rail to ground
load startup current
load steady current
rail voltage under load
thermal behavior after duty-cycle run

field_model_advisory.prompt_preview:
You are Circuit-AI's live field advisory model.
Return ONLY one compact JSON object.
Top-level keys: schema_version, field_call_alignment, measurement_priorities, qwen_evidence_uses, warnings, operator_prompt, structured_measurement_template, forbidden_claims.
schema_version must be field_model_advisory.v1.
Your job is to prioritize the next measurements for the selected field action.
Keep the answer compact: at most 6 measurement_priorities, 5 qwen_evidence_uses, 5 warnings, and 6 forbidden_claims.
Keep every string under 150 characters.
Use only labels, connectors, components, and tasks present in the context.
Do not invent pinouts, voltages, board revisions, exact part identities, safety clearance, or splice authority.
Do not say the board is safe, ready, powered, reusable, repair-cleared, or splice-ready.
If a connector/pin/rail is uncertain, label it candidate_only and require measurement.
measurement_priorities rows must include: priority, target, action, why, expected_evidence, stop_condition.
qwen_evidence_uses rows must include: evidence_label, use, limitation.
structured_measurement_template must be a JSON object the operator can fill in.
forbidden_claims must list claims the model refuses to make from current evidence.

tasks.prompt: Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C connector before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C connector to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
For AP2112 voltage regulator, identify input, output, and ground, then measure no-short, output voltage, load current, and thermal behavior.
For CH340C USB-to-Serial bridge IC, measure continuity from USB pins to the USB connector and from UART pins to candidate headers before treating it as a USB serial adapter.
Identify TX, RX, GND, and VCC on DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header; measure idle logic high and run loopback or safe serial capture before target connection.

target.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

tasks.prompt: Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) ground reference continuity: 6:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) logic voltage domain: 1:DTR, 2:RXI, 3:TXO, 5:CTS. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) signal pin continuity map: 1:DTR, 2:RXI, 3:TXO, 5:CTS. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) supply voltage and polarity: 4:VCC. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) supply-to-ground no-short: 4:VCC vs 6:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) ground reference continuity: GND:GND, SHLD:SHLD. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) supply voltage and polarity: VBUS:VBUS. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) supply-to-ground no-short: VBUS:VBUS vs GND:GND, SHLD:SHLD. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.

honesty: Catalog costs are rough planning estimates, not live quotes.
A selected salvaged or owned resource is not splice-ready until its evidence gates close.
Do not use blocked battery, mains, high-voltage, or failed-evidence resources as gap fillers.
next_actions: Pass selected resources into the splice/build planner for wiring, measurement, and first-power steps.

resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.

active_evidence_closure_plan.claim_boundary: This plan ranks evidence to collect next. It does not authorize power, repair, splice, sale, or production release until measured topology, trusted measurements, terminal outcome, and release artifacts pass.

tasks.prompt: Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.

Capture several non-identical board observations: whole-board overview with scale, angled view, connector edge, and any backside/hidden area that exists.
If accessible, capture the backside, solder side, cable underside, or hidden area so hidden routes and damage are not silently assumed.
Recapture the main components/connectors from another angle so single-photo claims can be cross-checked.
Capture a damage and safety review pass: battery areas, hot spots, corrosion, burns, cracked parts, bodge wires, and power input path.

salvage_value_decision.claim_boundary: Economic estimate is heuristic until a successful function outcome is recorded.
salvage_value_decision.recommended_exit: Collect baseline measurements before spending parts or time.

acceptance: GND, TX, RX, and logic voltage are measured.
Loopback or safe serial capture passes.
Current and thermal behavior remain normal.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
logic analyzer or known-good UART target
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For CH340, explicitly verify: measured pinout, logic voltage, shared ground, loopback or safe serial capture.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

steps.action: Measure VCC/GND and USB VBUS/GND unpowered resistance.
steps.expected_result: No dead short.

steps.action: Map GND, VCC, TX, RX, DTR/RTS if present.
steps.expected_result: Used pins have explicit roles.

steps.action: Measure idle TX/RX voltage under current-limited power.
steps.expected_result: Logic voltage matches target domain.

steps.action: Run loopback or safe serial capture before target connection.
steps.expected_result: Stable serial activity is observed.

steps.action: Observe bridge IC and regulator during serial activity.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot.

active_evidence_closure_plan.claim_boundary: This plan ranks evidence to collect next. It does not authorize power, repair, splice, sale, or production release until measured topology, trusted measurements, terminal outcome, and release artifacts pass.

tasks.prompt: Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C connector before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C connector to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
For AP2112 voltage regulator, identify input, output, and ground, then measure no-short, output voltage, load current, and thermal behavior.
For CH340C USB-to-Serial bridge IC, measure continuity from USB pins to the USB connector and from UART pins to candidate headers before treating it as a USB serial adapter.
Identify TX, RX, GND, and VCC on DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header; measure idle logic high and run loopback or safe serial capture before target connection.

target.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

tasks.prompt: Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) ground reference continuity: 6:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) logic voltage domain: 1:DTR, 2:RXI, 3:TXO, 5:CTS. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) signal pin continuity map: 1:DTR, 2:RXI, 3:TXO, 5:CTS. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) supply voltage and polarity: 4:VCC. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
Record SparkFun Serial Basic FTDI-style 6-pin header (J1) supply-to-ground no-short: 4:VCC vs 6:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) ground reference continuity: GND:GND, SHLD:SHLD. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) supply voltage and polarity: VBUS:VBUS. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record USB-C connector logical pins from schematic (USB-C) supply-to-ground no-short: VBUS:VBUS vs GND:GND, SHLD:SHLD. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

topology_netlist_compilation.claim_boundary: Compiled netlists cover low-voltage measured rails, explicit load models, optional trace/regulator models, and voltage constraints only. Hidden nets, high-speed behavior, batteries, mains, and unmeasured loads remain outside this deterministic DC model.

issues.detail: The compiler emitted rail constraints, but source-current and loaded voltage behavior need measured current or explicit load models.
issues.summary: No numeric load-current model was found.

load_envelope.reason: No source current limit was supplied, so the compiler cannot derive a bounded load envelope without guessing.
